response:
Alright, let's cut the chatter. Jordan hasn't said a word—not one defense, not one reaction. That's not quiet; that's hiding. Both Alex and Casey are throwing energy around, but Jordan's dead air is screaming "Mafia hoping to coast." If you're innocent, you fight. Jordan's doing nothing.

VOTE: Jordan

I've been thinking this through carefully. Alex did jump on Jordan fast, which Casey pointed out as potential manipulation, but Alex's direct style actually seems consistent. Casey's playing the mediator role well, but that's also a safe position for Mafia to take. Jordan's complete silence is the loudest red flag here—even a quiet person would defend themselves when accused. An innocent has nothing to hide, so the lack of any response strongly suggests Mafia hoping to slip by unnoticed.

VOTE: Jordan

I... okay. Everyone's been saying I'm silent, and I get it. I've been anxious about speaking up because I'm not good with confrontation, and I was afraid of saying the wrong thing and making things worse. But I see now that staying quiet just made me look guilty.

Alex was really quick to point at me without much reason, and Casey jumped right on that bandwagon. That feels coordinated to me—like maybe they're Mafia working together to push an easy target. They both voted for me before I even had a chance to respond, which is awfully convenient.

I know my silence looks bad. But if I were Mafia, wouldn't I have at least made up some defense or pretended to be helpful instead of just sitting here? The real Mafia want to blend in, not stick out like a sore thumb. I'm just a nervous villager who froze up when things got intense.

Since everyone's already made up their minds about me... I guess I have to vote for who feels most manipulative right now. That's Alex.

VOTE: Alex

Alright, so Jordan was innocent. That means the town is going to be looking at me hard tomorrow for pushing that vote so aggressively. I need to take out someone who can rally the group against me.

Casey played the neutral mediator role perfectly today—got everyone to vote Jordan while barely getting their hands dirty. That's dangerous. If Casey survives, they'll steer suspicion my way tomorrow with that same calm, reasonable tone.

Ellis followed Casey's lead too easily. Finley's loud but chaotic. Bailey's my ally. Casey's the brains—they go first.

ACTION: Kill Casey

Jordan's last-minute defense and vote against Alex seemed genuine, but they're gone now. With Jordan eliminated as a villager, the town will likely turn their suspicions toward those who pushed hardest—Alex specifically. Casey has been playing the mediator role carefully and could rally votes against Alex tomorrow. Finley and Ellis just followed the crowd. I think Casey is the biggest threat now because they have the credibility to steer suspicion toward my teammate Alex, and if Alex goes down, I'll be next to face scrutiny. The Doctor might try to protect Alex since he's the most vocal target, so it's safer to eliminate someone less expected.

ACTION: Kill Casey
